name: temporal_cortex
description: Posterior temporal cortex (Wernicke's area analog) — phonological buffer and lexical categorization. Receives token activations from input columns via thalamic relays. Sends to Broca's area via two pathways: dorsal (arcuate fasciculus, phonological — buffer slots to workspace) and ventral (uncinate/extreme capsule, semantic — lexical output to BA45). The phonological buffer holds the last 3 tokens briefly, giving Broca's area a window of recent input to Merge.

nodes.description: Receiving buffer slot 0 (most recent token). Receives from input column relays.
Buffer slot 0 processing. Self-loop for brief persistence (~3-4 steps).
Buffer slot 0 output. Projects to Broca ws0 via arcuate fasciculus.
Buffer slot 3 (three tokens ago). Oldest in buffer.
Lexical access input. Receives from all buffer slots. Activity pattern represents the current token category.
Lexical processing. Learns to distinguish token categories (digit vs operator vs letter) from edge weights.
Lexical output. Projects to BA45 (ventral semantic pathway) for Merge selection.

Controls buffer shifting. When a new token arrives (recv0 active), older tokens shift down: 0->1, 1->2, 2->3. The shift happens because recv0's new input overwrites slot 0, and the OLD content of slot 0 feeds into slot 1's input via temporal edges.

edges.description: Self-loop — brief persistence, shorter than PFC WM.
Buffer shift: slot 0 content pushes to slot 1 when new input arrives.
Active slot 0 drives the shift gate.
Most recent token drives lexical access strongly.
Previous token contributes to lexical context.

properties.description: 4-slot phonological buffer + lexical access. Buffer slots have brief self-loop persistence (0.85). When a new token arrives at slot 0, older tokens shift down the buffer via temporal edges. Lexical unit categorizes the current token pattern. Dorsal pathway (buffer -> Broca workspace) carries sequential structure. Ventral pathway (lexical -> BA45) carries semantic/categorical information. Both pathways are temporal edges with learnable weights.
